extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE attacked on the climbs of Stage 10 and won by over a minute, while VAN AVERMAET shockingly defended yellow by infiltrating the breakaway and finishing fourth despite not being a climber.
tactical_patterns: VAN AVERMAET conserved energy at stage start, bridged to breakaway at optimal moment after others spent themselves establishing it.
ALAPHILIPPE's margin of victory exceeded one minute despite being with elite climbers, suggesting he significantly underrode his capabilities earlier.
Team Sky controlled peloton with THOMAS as protected rider, yet VAN AVERMAET's five-minute breakaway buffer neutralized their tempo work entirely.
FUGLSANG explicitly stated Sky teams agreed not to take yellow yet, revealing GC contenders prefer Van Avermaet working than assuming defensive obligations.

rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: Finished fourth atop Colombière despite not being a climber, doubled lead over THOMAS to 2+ minutes through offensive yellow defense
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Strengthened throughout stage and won by over a minute, demonstrating superior mountain capacity to reputation and polka dot competitors
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Solid ride to 3:21 back, Team Sky controlled tempo but gained no time on leaders who escaped in break
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Lost over two minutes to VAN AVERMAET despite Sky's tempo control, relegated to chasing rather than gaining yellow
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: negative: Serious struggle on Colombière suggests unrecovered residual damage from cobblestone stage crash, exposed by first mountain day
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: neutral: Post-race comments reveal GC teams strategically avoiding yellow jersey work burden, Sky strongest team but Movistar three-captain approach stretched thin
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: Top 10 finish but no notable offensive action, mentioned as expected contender without delivering decisive move
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: Present in contenders group but shown struggling compared to Sky's strength demonstration
